title: Schema Migration Graph and Compatibility Policy
context: Tribunus has 20+ JSON schemas spanning multiple versions (v1, v2). As the schema registry grows, every schema change must be safe, predictable, and replayable. Without explicit compatibility rules, schema changes risk breaking consumers silently, orphaned schemas accumulate, and migration becomes ad-hoc.
decision:
Adopt the following schema evolution doctrine.

BACKWARD COMPATIBILITY RULES:
- Fields may be ADDED but never REMOVED from a published schema version.
- New fields MUST have defaults or be optional.
- Field types may be WIDENED (string→string|number) but never NARROWED.
- Required fields may never be added to an existing version.
- Enum values may be ADDED but never REMOVED or RENAMED.
- The schema $id must remain stable within a version.

FORWARD COMPATIBILITY RULES:
- Consumers MUST preserve unknown fields when reading and forwarding data.
- Unknown fields are stored but not interpreted.
- When a new schema version adds a field, consumers that preserved the unknown field automatically have the data.

UNKNOWN-FIELD HANDLING:
- Ingress (API/import): PRESERVE — store unknown fields for future use.
- Processing (internal): PRESERVE — don't strip fields you don't understand.
- Egress (export/serialize): PRESERVE — forward complete data.
- Validation: REJECT invalid fields but ACCEPT unknown fields.

CANONICAL SERIALIZATION:
- JSON with sorted keys (deterministic output).
- No trailing commas, 2-space indentation.
- Date-time fields use ISO 8601.
- Null values are omitted from output (not included as explicit null).

MIGRATION REPLAY:
- Migrations are versioned and committed alongside schema changes.
- Forward migration: apply schema changes from vN to vN+1.
- Reverse migration: rollback vN+1 to vN (must be explicitly defined).
- Migration replay: apply all migrations sequentially from genesis to current.
- Migrations are tested in CI: apply forward, verify, apply reverse, verify.

SCHEMA VERSION GRAPH:
- The schema index (schemas/index.json) tracks all schema versions.
- The governance section (added by ADR 0025 ownership model) tracks deprecation timelines.
- Schema versions form a directed graph: v1→v2, v1→v3 (fork), v2→v4 (evolution).
- The graph is queryable: which versions exist, which supersede which, which are deprecated.

COMPATIBILITY CHECKER:
- A CI step (schema:compat-check) validates:
  - No removed fields in same major version
  - No narrowed types
  - No added required fields
  - Schema $id stability
- The checker runs on every schema change PR.
- Breaks are surfaced as CI failures with specific field-level diagnostics.
consequences: Schema changes are now governed by explicit compatibility rules. Breaking changes require a new major version (v1→v2). The compatibility checker prevents silent consumer breakage. Unknown-field preservation ensures forward compatibility without coordination. Migration replay ensures every schema state is reproducible from genesis. The schema version graph provides a complete history of all schema evolution.
